response.justification: Candidate is an experienced senior software engineer with strong full-stack and systems credentials, but the role specifically demands a C++ developer with deep expertise in C++/STL, industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, and message-broker architectures. The resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (Dec 2014 – Jun 2015) over a decade ago, with no recent C++ work, no industrial cybersecurity exposure, and no IoT/automation protocol experience. Primary stacks are TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which do not map cleanly to the core requirements.
strengths: 10+ years of senior engineering experience with strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Prior low-latency, high-throughput systems work (DLang at Sociomantic handling millions of req/s) demonstrates performance engineering aptitude
Past C/C++ experience at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI, a measurement/engineering tool adjacent to industrial domains
concerns: C++ experience is over 10 years old and brief; no evidence of modern C++/STL proficiency at scale
No exposure to IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial cybersecurity standards
No demonstrated experience with MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, message brokers, or industrial automation/IoT platforms

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation / IEC 62443 / IoT protocol background
Strong senior-level engineering fundamentals and performance systems track record
